vulnerability.title: Prevent remote hosts from connecting to the proxy display
vulnerability.description: The SSH daemon should prevent remote hosts from connecting to the proxy display. The default SSH configuration for X11UseLocalhost is yes, which prevents remote hosts from connecting to the proxy display. To explicitly prevent remote connections to the proxy display, add or correct the following line in /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf: X11UseLocalhost yes
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then mkdir -p /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d touch /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf chmod 0600 /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf LC_ALL=C sed -i "/^\s*X11UseLocalhost\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config" LC_ALL=C sed -i "/^\s*X11UseLocalhost\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d"/*.conf if [ -e "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf" ] ; then LC_ALL=C sed -i "/^\s*X11UseLocalhost\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf" else touch "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf" fi # make sure file has newline at the end sed -i -e '$a\' "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/0

triage_decision.reason: This is a standard SSH hardening configuration change that ensures X11 forwarding is restricted to the local machine. It does not impact core SSH connectivity or authentication, only the security of X11 proxying.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: May break X11 forwarding for remote users if they specifically require remote X11 access

previous_attempts.scan_output: Single-rule scan failed for sshd_x11_use_localhost (rule=xccdf_org.ssgproject.content_rule_sshd_x11_use_localhost). Could not verify.

commands_executed: echo "X11UseLocalhost yes" > /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf && chmod 0600 /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf && systemctl reload sshd

per_turn.command: echo "X11UseLocalhost yes" > /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf && chmod 0600 /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf && systemctl reload sshd

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The plan is sound and follows a logical sequence to ensure the hardening configuration is applied both in the main config and in the include directory. It correctly addresses potential conflicts in the main sshd_config file.
concerns: The plan does not explicitly mention restarting or reloading the sshd service, which is necessary for changes to take effect.
suggested_improvements: Add a step to reload the SSH daemon (e.g., 'systemctl reload sshd') after the configuration changes are verified.
Add a syntax check step using 'sshd -t' before restarting the service to prevent accidental lockout due to configuration errors.
